skill.name: ppt-editing-skill
skill.description: Edit existing PowerPoint files and templates through a structured PPTX XML workflow. The skill guides template analysis, slide mapping, slide duplication or deletion, XML-safe content edits, cleanup, and validated repacking.
skill.summary: Edit template-based PowerPoint decks with a careful PPTX XML workflow.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, path traversal, and weak-crypto patterns. Manual review found the high-severity alerts are false positives from Markdown examples, XML placeholders, and descriptive text, with no evidence of malicious intent or data exfiltration. The remaining risk is low and comes from local shell commands and filesystem writes used for PPTX editing.

low_findings.title: Local shell command workflow
low_findings.description: The skill documents local commands such as copying a user-provided PPTX and running markitdown. These are legitimate presentation-editing steps, but users should treat file paths and source PPTX files as untrusted inputs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and local-only. Confidence is high that this is a real low-level operational risk, not malicious code execution.

low_findings.title: Local PPTX filesystem writes
low_findings.description: The workflow creates template, unpacked, and edited presentation files and recommends writing to /tmp before copying to the final path. This is expected for PPTX editing, but it can overwrite files if paths are chosen poorly.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write behavior is directly described in the skill. There is no evidence of hidden persistence or unauthorized file access.

low_findings.title: Static high-risk alerts appear to be false positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-crypto and path traversal alerts map to Markdown text, XML examples, and ellipsis placeholders. No evidence found of cryptographic code, traversal against user-controlled paths, or malicious prompt injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines shows descriptive prose and XML formatting guidance, not executable cryptographic or traversal logic.

content.value_statement: Template-based presentation updates often break layouts, relationships, and embedded assets. This skill provides a structured workflow for editing PPTX files through validated XML changes.
actual_capabilities: Guides copying and analyzing a user-provided PPTX template before editing.
Maps source content to varied slide layouts to avoid repetitive deck design.
Explains safe slide deletion, duplication, and reordering through PPTX structure.
Provides XML editing rules for text, bullets, paragraphs, and smart quotes.
Recommends cleanup and validation steps before repacking the edited deck.
limitations: It is an instruction-only skill and does not include the referenced helper scripts.
It assumes the user has tools such as markitdown and PPTX processing scripts available.
It does not guarantee visual perfection after long text replacements or layout changes.
It should only process presentations from trusted or reviewed sources.

use_cases.title: Update client proposal decks
use_cases.description: Replace template placeholders with client-specific content while preserving slide layouts and presentation structure.

use_cases.title: Refresh internal reporting templates
use_cases.description: Adapt a recurring PowerPoint report with new sections, reordered slides, and cleaned unused assets.

use_cases.title: Prepare production-ready slide files
use_cases.description: Edit PPTX XML carefully, validate extracted content, and repack a final deck for delivery.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a deck template
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to analyze my PowerPoint template. Identify the available slide layouts, placeholder text, and likely content slots.

prompt_templates.title: Map content to slides
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to map my source content to the template slides. Choose varied layouts and explain which slides should be duplicated or removed.

prompt_templates.title: Edit slide content safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to update the slide XML with my final content. Preserve bullets, paragraph structure, smart quotes, and existing formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Rebuild and validate the deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to clean orphaned assets, repack the edited PPTX, validate the output text, and list any layout risks that need manual review.

output_examples.input: A proposal template with placeholder slides and a new client brief.
output_examples.output: A slide mapping plan that names reused layouts, deleted placeholders, duplicated sections, and content assigned to each slide.

output_examples.input: A draft deck where several text boxes need longer replacement copy.
output_examples.output: A concise edit plan that flags overflow risks, recommends split paragraphs, and preserves template formatting rules.

output_examples.input: An edited PPTX that needs final cleanup before delivery.
output_examples.output: A validation checklist covering orphaned slides, unused media, extracted text review, and final repacking status.

best_practices: Work on a copied PPTX file and keep the original template unchanged.
Finish slide structure changes before editing individual slide XML files.
Validate the final deck with extracted text and visual inspection before delivery.
anti_patterns: Do not reuse one basic bullet layout for every slide in a deck.
Do not clear unwanted template elements without removing their full visual group.
Do not concatenate multiple list items into one paragraph inside slide XML.

faq.question: Does this skill create presentations from scratch?
faq.answer: It is designed for editing existing PowerPoint files or templates, not for building a full deck from a blank file.

faq.question: Can it preserve the original deck?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow starts by copying the user-provided PPTX before making any edits.

faq.question: Does it include the helper scripts it mentions?
faq.answer: No. The skill references helper scripts, but this package contains only the instruction file.

faq.question: Why does it edit PPTX XML directly?
faq.answer: Direct XML editing can preserve template structure when changes are careful and validated.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential presentations?
faq.answer: The documented workflow is local, but users should follow their organization policies for sensitive files.

faq.question: What should be checked before delivery?
faq.answer: Review extracted text, slide order, removed assets, text overflow, and the final presentation in PowerPoint.
